message:
So I was making dinner last week and set off the smoke alarm with some slightly-too-ambitious pan-searing. My teenage kid yells from upstairs, 'MOM, IS THAT THE SMOKE ALARM OR JUST YOU COOKING AGAIN?' 😅

It was funny... until I realized we'd never actually talked about what we'd do if it was a real fire. Like, where would everyone go? Do we have a meeting spot? I was standing there with a spatula feeling very unprepared.

Turns out a lot of families are in that boat—we *have* smoke alarms (check!), but the plan part? That lives in the vague cloud of "we should probably do that sometime."

I sat everyone down that same evening and we mapped out exits, picked a spot in the front yard, even tested it like a drill. Took maybe 20 minutes. And honestly? It felt less like a chore and more like we'd actually covered something that matters.

Have you and your family ever done an actual fire escape walkthrough together, or is that still on the someday list? 🏠
